results.title: Program Manager - Compliance, GT Cybersecurity & Risk Management - Expeditors | Built In Seattle
highlights:
# Program Manager - Compliance, GT Cybersecurity & Risk Management
[...]
the Cybersecurity & Risk Management
[...]
compliance initiatives,
[...]
The Program Manager - IS Compliance serves as a high-impact position in the Cybersecurity and Risk Management Portfolio in the Global Technology (GT) department as a thought leader that bridges the gap between traditional financial compliance and a growing technology environment, interfacing with 1st Line process owners, our company's Internal Audit department, as well as external auditors, with the goal of demonstrating compliance with our system of internal control, empowering and accelerating GT department Compliance programs through industry standard compliance methodologies and providing systematic visibility to health and performance. The ideal candidate will have a strong depth of understanding of compliance and audit fundamentals, the COSO and COBIT frameworks and Internal Controls over financial reporting, with multiple years detailed Audit experience (CISA certification preferred). Additionally, this role will be filled by an excellent communicator with detailed project management and analytic capabilities, with the ability to communicate critical audit matters to management, identify trends and remediate and avoid issues.
[...]
- Deep knowledge of compliance frameworks (SOX, COBIT). Advanced understanding of technical IT domains (Operating Systems, Databases, Networks). Experience with complex IT control testing and validation.
[...]
- Strong data analytics and visualization skills. Ability to curate and maintain complex audit artifacts and system configurations.
[...]
- Primary liaison for external auditors, handling complex technical inquiries and walkthroughs. Translates for 1st Line technical teams to interpret audit requirements and design compliant controls.
[...]
- Leads root cause analysis discussions and present technical solutions. Independently manages audit programs and drives projects to completion without direct supervision.
[...]
- Design controls that are "compliant by design." Propose technical solutions based on deep-dive investigations into control failures.
[...]
- Creates and maintains the integrity of audit artifacts and system configurations.
[...]
- Ownership of end-to-end audit programs for high-risk or complex technical areas. Strategic approach to identifying root causes and implementing sustainable solutions.
[...]
- Bachelor's degree in Information Systems, Accounting, Business, or a relevant field.
- 4-6 years of detailed IS audit experience.
- Strong understanding of SOX, COBIT, and technical IT domains (e.g., OS, DB, Network).
- Ability to work autonomously and drive projects to completion without direct supervision.
- Strong data analytics and visualization skills.
- Certification: CISA, CPA, or CIA preferred.
[...]
Lead product vision and strategy for security operations services, defining outcomes for managed investigation, response, threat hunting, and advisory services. Partner with customer security ops, product, and marketing teams to deliver AI-driven, automated features, represent Zscaler to customers, and drive measurable security outcomes and operational alignment.
[...]
Top Skills: Agentic WorkflowsAi/MlAutomationGenerative AiMcpSecurity Data LakeSecurity Operations Center (Soc)Threat Detection And ResponseZero Trust
[...]

- Major Tech Employers: Amazon, Microsoft, Meta, Google
- Key Industries: Artificial intelligence, cloud computing, software, biotechnology, game development
- Funding Landscape: $3.1 billion in venture capital funding in 2024 (Pitchbook)
[...]

[...]

results.title: Expeditors details customs growth, AI and trade impacts | EXPD 8-K Filing
highlights:
The company highlights growing customs brokerage demand driven by complex tariffs, IEEPA-related refunds, and other post-entry work, and says technology, including AI, is improving productivity and margins without replacing human compliance judgment. Expeditors also describes strong hyperscaler-related airfreight demand tied to AI data center construction and reiterates capital priorities of investing for organic growth and returning excess cash through dividends and share repurchases.
[...]
Certain portions of this document contain forward-looking statements, which are based on certain assumptions and expectations of future events that are subject to risks and uncertainties. These forward-looking statements include statements regarding any direct or indirect impact on us, our customers, or our service providers from conflict in the Middle East or elsewhere; no sign of recovery in the ocean market due to the ongoing imbalance of global capacity versus demand and the impact on volumes and pricing; our ability to use and benefit from our investments in technology, including AI; our belief that refund activity and other post-entry activity will continue and are likely to lead to a significant increase in our customs work; our ability to enhance our productivity and profitability when adding headcount to our customs group or any part of our business; our ability to continue to benefit from increases in hyperscaler volumes, with growth in AI data center construction expected to peak by the end of the decade; and generate sustainable, profitable and capital-efficient growth over the long term. These and other factors are discussed in the CompanyŌĆÖs regulatory filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission, including those in ŌĆ£Item 1A. Risk FactorsŌĆØ of the CompanyŌĆÖs Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, and the CompanyŌĆÖs most recent Form 10-Q. The forward-looking statements contained in this filing speak only as of this date and the Company does not assume any obligation to update them except as required by law.
[...]
Because customs work requires significant experience and oversight, we have focused much of our technology investments, including AI, on that work. We are starting to achieve benefits from those investments, applying technology to enhance productivity and operating margins without displacing compliance judgment, risk management, or accountability, which are core to our value proposition. As we have said before, technology can assist and enhance the customs process; it cannot replace it. Our ability to process higher entry volumes more quickly and efficiently supports revenue growth and can enhance margins. Also keep in mind that the Customs and other business also includes import services, Transcon ground transportation, and warehousing and distribution services, which all grew at double-digits during the first quarter.
[...]
Comments from our hyperscaler customers generally align with what we read in industry reports suggesting that the unprecedented demand for AI data center construction could peak by the end of the decade. While that may be true, what we see is that volumes for this industry are holding strong and we currently are seeing continued increases in volume, both from new servers and to replace aging servers. As data farms are popping up all over the globe and hyperscaler investments continue, we are shipping products for most of the big names in AI across the U.S., to India, Southeast Asia, Europe, and the Middle East.
[...]
. Our significant
[...]
have a positive
[...]
Expeditors is directing much of its technology and AI investment toward customs work to enhance productivity and operating margins. The company states that technology supports faster, more efficient processing of higher entry volumes while preserving human compliance judgment, risk management, and accountability in the customs process.

results.title: Is Expeditors (EXPD) Using Automation Layoffs to Rewire Its Long-Term Efficiency Story? - Simply Wall St News
highlights:
- Expeditors International of Washington recently cut 230 technology jobs in the Seattle area, with CEO Daniel Wall linking the reductions to automation and new digital tools that are reshaping operations.
- This shift highlights how Expeditors is reconfiguring its workforce and cost base around automation, even as the broader trade and compliance landscape becomes more complex.
- Next, we will examine how ExpeditorsŌĆÖ push toward automation-led efficiency reshapes its investment narrative for long-term-focused investors.
[...]
To own Expeditors, you have to be comfortable with a company that pairs a disciplined balance sheet and high return on equity with a premium valuation and relatively modest forecast growth. The recent decision to cut 230 technology roles in Seattle, framed by management as a byproduct of automation, fits into an efficiency push that could support margins but also introduces short term execution and cultural risks just as trade and compliance demands are getting tougher. With the share price still not far from consensus fair value and coming off a very strong 1 year total return, the key near term catalysts remain how volumes, yields and buybacks interact with this automation drive, and whether cost cuts start to show up in reported results without disrupting service quality.
[...]
However, this efficiency story also sits alongside rising concerns about cost cuts affecting long term competitiveness and culture. Expeditors International of Washington's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.
[...]
- Uncover the next big thing with 24 elite penny stocks that balance risk and reward.
- The future of work is here. Discover the 33 top robotics and automation stocks leading the charge in AI-driven automation and industrial transformation.
- AI is about to change healthcare. These 39 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.
[...]
#### Expeditors International of Washington
[...]
Provides logistics services in the Americas, North Asia, South Asia, Europe, and Middle East, Africa, and India.

AI Developer ŌĆō Container & Logistics Industry | Hirevector

# AI Developer ŌĆō Container & Logistics Industry

Posted Jun 6, 2026

We are seeking a skilled AI Developer to help modernise and optimise operations across the container and logistics ecosystem. You will build intelligent systems that enhance container tracking, route optimization, yard operations, demand forecasting, predictive maintenance, and supply chain visibility. This role focuses on applying AI to real-world shipping and containerized freight challenges ŌĆö improving operational efficiency, reducing costs, and increasing transparency across global logistics networks. What You Will Be Doing: Design and deploy AI/ML models for: Container tracking and ETA prediction Route and load optimization Yard and terminal operations automation Predictive maintenance for container fleets and equipment Demand forecasting and capacity planning Develop real-time analytics systems for supply chain monitoring Integrate AI solutions with TMS (Transportation Management Systems), WMS (Warehouse Management Systems), and IoT devices Work with telematics, GPS, RFID, and sensor data Build APIs and scalable services to support operational platforms Collaborate with operations, logistics planners, and engineering teams Ensure data security and compliance across global shipping regulations Optimise models for production-scale performance About You: BachelorŌĆÖs or MasterŌĆÖs degree in Computer Science, AI, Data Science, or related field 3+ years of experience in AI/ML development Strong Python programming skills Experience with machine learning frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn) Experience working with time-series data and large operational datasets Knowledge of optimization algorithms (linear programming, heuristic optimization) Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) Familiarity with REST APIs and microservices architecture Understanding of MLOps and model deployment Your Qualifications: Experience in logistics, shipping, freight, or container operations Familiarity with supply chain analytics and transportation systems Experience with IoT data processing Knowledge of geospatial analytics (GIS tools, mapping APIs) Experience building real-time streaming systems (Kafka, Spark, etc.) Understanding of global trade operations and port logistics Experience working in distributed, remote teams What We Offer: Fully remote, flexible work environment Competitive salary with performance incentives Opportunity to work on AI systems impacting global trade and logistics Access to large-scale operational datasets Professional development and industry exposure Collaborative, cross-functional international team Impact YouŌĆÖll Make: Improve container turnaround time Reduce empty container repositioning costs Increase supply chain visibility Optimize port and yard efficiency Support sustainable and data-driven logistics operations

highlights:
AI Engineer, Global Operations | Jobspawn

Jobspawn.ŌåÉ All Jobs

# AI Engineer, Global Operations

Posted Jun 1, 2026

At Iron Mountain we know that work, when done well, makes a positive impact for our customers, our employees, and our planet. ThatŌĆÖs why we need smart, committed people to join us. Whether youŌĆÖre looking to start your career or make a change, talk to us and see how you can elevate the power of your work at Iron Mountain.

We provide expert, sustainable solutions in records and information management, digital transformation services, data centers, asset lifecycle management, and fine art storage, handling, and logistics. We proudly partner every day with our 225,000 customers around the world to preserve their invaluable artifacts, extract more from their inventory, and protect their data privacy in innovative and socially responsible ways.

Are you curious about being part of our growth storŌĆŗy while evolving your skills in a culture that will welcome your unique contributions? If so, let's start the conversation.

Job Summary

Iron Mountain is seeking a dynamic and experienced AI Engineer to join our Operations team. In this role, you will be responsible for designing and deploying production-ready Machine Learning models and Artificial Intelligence agents that optimize complex logistics workflows and drive measurable cost reductions.

You will join a high-impact team dedicated to bridging the gap between operational business challenges and scalable technical solutions, directly influencing our supply chain and field operations.

What YouŌĆÖll Do

- In this role, you will:
- Develop End-to-End AI Solutions: Design, build, and monitor production-ready Machine Learning models and automated agents to streamline multi-step operational decision-making.
- Optimize Operational Workflows: Collaborate with Finance, Facilities, and Information Technology teams to build robust Extract, Transform, Load (ETL) pipelines and integrate predictive tools into existing warehouse and logistics software.
- Govern AI Safety and Compliance: Ensure the responsible deployment of all systems by performing rigorous model validation, maintaining strict data governance, and mitigating risk or bias in automated decisions.

What YouŌĆÖll Bring

- The ideal candidate will have:
- 5+ years of applied experience in Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning engineering or advanced analytics, preferably within logistics, supply chain, or transportation industries.
- Advanced technical expertise in Python and SQL, with proven experience scaling "agentic" workflows and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) solutions using platforms like LangChain or n8n.
- Proven ability in strategic problem-solving and rapid prototyping, with the communication skills necessary to explain complex model outputs to non-technical business leaders.
- A BachelorŌĆÖs degree in a quantitative field such as Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, or Mathematics.
- United States Citizenship is required.

- What We Offer
- Competitive compensation and benefits aligned with your experience level.
- Flexible work options to support a healthy workŌĆōlife balance.
- Comprehensive health, wellness, and retirement plans to support your long-term well-being.
- Opportunities for continuous learning and professional growth in a cutting-edge technical environment.

Reasonably expected salary range: $107,500.00 - $143,300.00

Please note that an employee's starting salary may vary based on a variety of factors. Where State, Municipal, Provincial, Territorial or other legal minimum wages exceed the federal minimum wage, employees are entitled to the higher rate.

results.title: Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery) - Xtalks
highlights:
Machine Learning Engineer
[...]
## Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery)
[...]
A healthier future. It's what drives us to innovate. To continuously advance science and ensure everyone has access to the healthcare they need today and for generations to come. Creating a world where we all have more time with the people we love. That's what makes us Roche.Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche's Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness this transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide.The OpportunityAt Roche's AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning techniques. We are seeking talented engineers with a passion for building large-scale, distributed machine learning algorithms and systems that will transform the drug discovery process. AIDD's Foundation Model team is seeking an exceptional Machine Learning Engineer to develop the backend of our internal agents platform and enable the next generation of AI/ML-powered drug discovery applications as part of our broader Lab-in-the-Loop approach. We are looking for someone who is not only passionate about technical problem-solving but also has a proven track record of delivering innovative solutions in AI/ML. The ideal candidate should have extensive experience in leading and managing a team of engineers and scientists, and be able to foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and excellence across the broader Roche organization. The group provides a dynamic and challenging environment for multidisciplinary research, including access to heterogeneous data sources, close links to top academic institutions around the world, as well as collaborations with internal Genentech and Roche teams. In this role, you will:
[...]
- Contact
- Develop various, cutting-edge internal AI/ML-based applications for drug discovery and development.
[...]

# Generative AI - Senior Associate Job Opening in New York, New York - Crains New York
[...]
Generative AI - Senior Associate Job Opening in New York, New York - Crains New York

Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
Generative artificial intelligence is transforming how we build products, serve clients, and operate at scale. In the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help turn advanced models into dependable, secure, and high-performing production services. You will work with partners across machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering to deliver solutions with clear return on investment. If you enjoy hands-on engineering, real-world constraints, and high-impact delivery, this role is for you. As a Senior Associate, Generative AI Engineer in the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help design, build, and support production generative artificial intelligence products and reusable backend application programming interfaces used across the firm. You will combine large enterprise datasets with large language and multimodal models to deliver scalable, measurable solutions. You will collaborate closely with machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering partners to ensure reliability, performance, and strong operational controls. You will contribute to technical design decisions, delivery planning, and continuous improvement of our platforms and products.Job responsibilities
